name: Zucchini, Leek, and Chevre Tart in Wild Rice Crust
recipeIngredient: 2 cups thinly sliced leek (including some stalk)
1 tablespoon chopped fresh marjoram or savory
recipeInstructions:
For a zucchini quiche with a crunchy wild rice crust, try Wehani. This recipe offers a satisfying counterpoint to the creamy French custard filling. Allow time for the rice to cook and cool before assembling.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
To make the crust:
1. Beat the egg, cheese, lemon juice, and melted butter together in a bowl until combined.
2. Stir in the cooked wild rice, seasoning with salt and pepper as desired. Transfer mixture to a 9-inch pie pan and press evenly over the bottom and sides.
3. Bake until set and crisp, about 15 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature, approximately 30 minutes. Alternatively, cover tightly and refrigerate for up to overnight; return to room temperature before adding the filling.
Meanwhile, prepare the filling:
1. Place zucchini in a colander over a bowl or sink. Generously salt and mix with fingertips to distribute the salt evenly. Let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Gather zucchini and gently squeeze out any excess moisture. Set aside.
3. Heat butter in a sauté pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add leek and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Add drained zucchini and continue cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
5. In a bowl, combine eggs, cream, Dijon mustard, crumbled chevre cheese, salt, pepper, and marjoram (if using). Whisk to blend well.
6. Stir in the sautéed leek and zucchini mixture.
7. Pour into the prepared rice crust and bake at 350°F for about 30-35 minutes until filling is set and golden.
Serve hot or at room temperature.
